name: Marvin Stamm
abstract: Marvin Louis Stamm (born May 23, 1939) is an American jazz trumpeter.

concerts.label: Concert of Music by Meyer Kupferman
JVC Jazz Festival: Dave Brubeck Quartet and Big Band/ Bill Cosby
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

concerts.label: Martin Erickson: A Recital and Celebration of the Modern Tuba

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: I was involved with something called the American Jazz Orchestra, with John Lewis... And this was a band comprised of mostly big band veterans: Marvin Stamm...
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: They had Tootie Heath on drums, Marvin Stamm on trumpet, Clifford on tenor and myself on bass
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Mays
about_quotes.evidence: And the project included Marvin Stamm, my wonderful friend and great trumpet player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Mays interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 5, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Gap Mangione
about_quotes.evidence: That band consisted of Snooky Young, Lew Soloff, Jon Faddis. That was the trumpet section — oh and Marvin Stamm.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gap Mangione interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rochester, New York, January 11, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: Having performed with him with Duke Pearson’s big band, I became highly aware of his vast talent.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: Here’s a big date with Hino for Flying Disc for Japan with a string section, and Marvin Stamm... That is a Hino production, production date that I probably just came and overdubbed.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
